title: High-Rate Free-Space Continuous-Variable QKD with Self-Referenced Passive State Preparation
authors: H. W. Yin, X. J. Liao, Y. H. Xu, P. Huang, K. T. Zhu, T. Wang, G. H. Zeng
abstract: Continuous-variable quantum key distribution (CVQKD) using passive state preparation (PSP) offers low-cost, high-rate secure communication. However, the existing PSP-CVQKD scheme with a transmitted local oscillator has high photon leakage noise and poor stability, making it unsuitable for high-loss transmission. In this work, for the first time, we propose and implement a local local oscillator (LLO) CVQKD system using a self-referenced (SR) PSP scheme, and give a theoretical proof of the equivalence of the PSP and GMCS protocol using temporal-mode theory. By employing the novel self-referenced pilot scheme to achieve high-precision time-varying frequency and phase compensation algorithms, we significantly improve the system' s signal-to-noise ratio and stability. The system achieves a record-high asymptotic secret key rate of 10.34 Mbps over a free-space channel with up to 23.5 dB loss, while maintaining low excess noise and robust performance under turbulent conditions. This work establishes the feasibility of SR-LLO CVQKD, providing a practical pathway toward secure, high-rate quantum communication in realistic environments.
summary: The researchers developed a new CVQKD method using a self-referenced local local oscillator to overcome noise and instability in free-space communication. They achieved a record-breaking 10.34 Mbps key rate even under significant channel loss. This provides a scalable and practical pathway for high-speed quantum secure communication in real-world environments.
main_problem: Existing continuous-variable quantum key distribution (CVQKD) schemes using transmitted local oscillators suffer from high photon leakage noise and instability, limiting performance in high-loss free-space channels.
main_result: The authors implemented a self-referenced local local oscillator (SR-LLO) CVQKD system that achieves a record-high asymptotic secret key rate of 10.34 Mbps over a free-space channel with up to 23.5 dB loss.
method: The work uses a self-referenced passive state preparation (PSP) scheme combined with a novel pilot scheme for high-precision time-varying frequency and phase compensation, supported by a theoretical proof using temporal-mode theory.
model_or_system: A continuous-variable quantum key distribution system operating over a free-space channel subject to atmospheric turbulence and high loss.
key_observables: Asymptotic secret key rate, excess noise, and signal-to-noise ratio (SNR).
important_parameters: 23.5 dB channel loss, 10.34 Mbps secret key rate.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of noise in transmitted LO schemes, proposes a self-referenced LLO-CVQKD architecture, provides a theoretical equivalence proof using temporal-mode theory, and demonstrates experimental performance in free-space turbulence.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant to quantum optics and quantum networks as it addresses fundamental challenges in maintaining coherence and stability in high-rate, long-distance quantum communication links.
